questions.question: What are the three sub-atomic particles of an atom?
questions.explanation: Atoms consist of these three fundamental particles that differ in mass and charge.

questions.question: Who discovered the electron?
questions.explanation: He identified the first sub-atomic particle using cathode ray experiments.

questions.question: What was the main conclusion of Rutherford’s alpha-particle scattering experiment?
questions.correct_answer: Most of the space inside the atom is empty and there is a small, dense, positively charged nucleus at the center
questions.explanation: Since most particles passed straight through while a few rebounded, he concluded the mass is concentrated in a tiny center.

questions.question: What are Bohr’s discrete orbits called?
questions.explanation: They are represented by the letters K, L, M, N,... or the numbers n=1, 2, 3, 4,...

questions.question: State the maximum number of electrons that can be accommodated in a shell.
questions.explanation: Where 'n' is the orbit number or energy level index.

questions.question: Define valency.
questions.correct_answer: The combining capacity of an atom
questions.explanation: It is determined by the number of valence electrons present in the outermost shell.

questions.question: What is the atomic number (Z)?
questions.correct_answer: The total number of protons present in the nucleus of an atom
questions.explanation: It is the unique identity of an element.

questions.question: What is the mass number (A)?
questions.correct_answer: The sum of the total number of protons and neutrons in the nucleus
questions.explanation: Protons and neutrons together are also called nucleons.

questions.question: What are isotopes?
questions.correct_answer: Atoms of the same element having the same atomic number but different mass numbers
questions.explanation: They have identical chemical properties but different physical properties.

questions.question: What are isobars?
questions.correct_answer: Atoms of different elements with different atomic numbers, which have the same mass number
questions.explanation: Example: Calcium (Z=20) and Argon (Z=18) both having mass number 40.

notes.title: Thomson's Model
notes.content: An atom consists of a positively charged sphere with electrons embedded in it.

notes.title: Bohr's Postulate
notes.content: While revolving in discrete orbits, electrons do not radiate energy.

notes.title: Neutron Properties
notes.content: Neutrons have no charge and mass nearly equal to that of a proton; they are absent in Hydrogen (Protium).

facts.fact: The mass of an electron is about 1/2000 times that of a proton.
The outermost shell of an atom can accommodate a maximum of 8 electrons (Octet Rule).
An isotope of Uranium is used as a fuel in nuclear reactors.
